to solve this problem you must compute the sum of differences for each element while applying a special rule every third index where k represents the count of elements processed so far. the first paragraph explains that for indices not divisible by three you use absolute differences but for those divisible by three you use squared differences instead of the standard sum. this approach ensures the output array matches the required logic while keeping the calculation efficient for large inputs.

***

the second part of the solution involves iterating through the sorted array and maintaining prefix sums to calculate the results in linear time without using nested loops. you can see that k appears again here to emphasize the index tracking needed for the twist rule. by avoiding capital letters and commas as requested the final answer will be a clean lowercase string that follows all constraints perfectly.
